Class CombatController.ReadWriteServer

java.lang.Object
controller.CombatController.ReadWriteServer
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
Enclosing class:
CombatController
public class CombatController.ReadWriteServer extends Object implements Runnable
ReadWriteServer handles the clients networking logic. It is created by connectToServer and runs until the game ends. The combat controller is updated based on the information received from the server and the view is updated when new objects are being read from the server.
Author:
Isa Abdulrazaq, Matt Lagier

  • Method Details

    • setP1

      public void setP1(Player p1)
      Sets the objects player 1 to a provided Player object. Used to update the thread using the controllers Player object.
      Parameters:
      p1 - Player object to replace p1 in the thread.

    • setP2

      public void setP2(Player p2)
      Sets the objects player 2 to a provided Player object. Used to update the thread using the controllers Player object.
      Parameters:
      p2 - Player object to replace p2 in the thread.

    • getp1

      public Player getp1()
      Returns the player 1 value stored in the thread.
      Returns:
      Player object stored in player 1

    • getp2

      public Player getp2()
      Returns the player 2 object stored in the thread.
      Returns:
      Player objected stored in player 2

    • setPlayerTurn

      public void setPlayerTurn(boolean turn)
      Set the player turn, used to update the thread using the combat controllers value.
      Parameters:
      turn - boolean value the turn should be.

    • getPlayerTurn

      public boolean getPlayerTurn()
      Returns the players turn boolean.
      Returns:

    • setPlayerID

      public void setPlayerID(int playerID)
      Sets the player ID, used during the connection process.
      Parameters:
      playerID - integer value representing the player ID.

    • changeTurn

      public void changeTurn(boolean changeTurn)
      Change the current turn, used to update the turn from the controllers value.
      Parameters:
      changeTurn - True/False representing the players turn.

    • run

      public void run()
      run contains the logic the thread will operate on. First the thread sends the current players object to the server based on their player ID. Then, depending on the ID, it reads, saves and updates the view or sends the current state of the game to the server.
      Specified by:
      run in interface Runnable
